physics_2sigma.description: 2σ anomaly on li_rate, betap_rate, mhd_rate, dalpha_rate, ne_rate
physics_2sigma.note: High detection + long warning BUT high false alarm rate

physics_3sigma.note: Low false alarm but misses 72% of disruptions

ml_efit_only_9var.note: EFIT equilibrium params alone give almost no warning time

ml_efit_fast_12var.note: ML with wrong labels (last 30%) does NOT help warning time

ml_confirmation.note: ML confirms too few — suppresses true alarms because labels dont match physics

key_findings.1_fast_diag_essential: MHD n=2 and li_rate provide 100-700ms lead time that EFIT cannot see
key_findings.2_physics_beats_ml: Physics 2σ anomaly (84% det, 195ms) vastly outperforms ML (16% det, 30ms)
key_findings.3_label_mismatch: ML fails because pre-disruption labels (last 30%) dont correspond to physical precursor onset
key_findings.4_false_alarm_tradeoff: 84% detection requires 72% false alarm — this is the physics-imposed limit with 0D+3fast signals
key_findings.5_causal_value: Causal model adds value NOT as ML filter but as EXPLANATION: when physics alarm fires, causal graph explains WHY

per_shot_signal_analysis.mhd_n2: 100-630ms lead (MHD mode growth is earliest precursor)

honest_assessment.what_works: Fast diagnostics provide 100-700ms lead time that EFIT misses entirely
honest_assessment.what_doesnt: ML on timepoint labels doesn't improve over physics thresholds
honest_assessment.vs_frnn: FRNN achieves ~87% det @ ~300ms using 400x more data + 1D profiles — we are at 84% @ 195ms with 60x less data but higher FA
honest_assessment.operational: 72% FA rate is not operational — needs locked mode + radiation + proper event-based labeling
honest_assessment.value_proposition: Physics anomaly detection + causal explanation is the right architecture for a safety advisory layer
